header.name: Sean Luka Girgis
header.employer_address:
Hilltop Securities
717 N Harwood St
Dallas, TX 75201

salutation: Dear Tim McKelvey and Hilltop Securities Hiring Team,
intro: I am interested in the Data Engineer – Risk Analytics contract role with Hilltop Securities. My background is a strong fit for building a risk management database, automating manual reporting, and translating financial/risk requirements into scalable data models, SQL/Python pipelines, and stakeholder-ready dashboards.
body: At Citi, I built automated Python and Pandas ETL pipelines that ingested and standardized telemetry from 6,000+ enterprise endpoints, replacing manual reporting workflows with repeatable, auditable data flows. I also designed Oracle schemas, Redshift-backed reporting structures, validation checks, and forecasting workflows used for capacity risk analysis, executive reporting, and operational decision-making in a regulated banking environment.

The Hilltop role's focus on risk analytics, financial data, trading activity, automated reporting, scalable infrastructure, and single-source-of-truth data flows maps closely to my strongest experience. I have deep hands-on experience with SQL, Python, Oracle, Redshift, AWS S3/Glue patterns, data modeling, data quality, and turning complex stakeholder requirements into technical models, dashboards, and operational reporting outputs.

I would bring a senior, practical data engineering approach: understand the risk reporting objective, design the database and data model, build auditable ETL/ELT flows, validate data integrity, automate reporting, and communicate clearly with Risk Managers and business users. I am prepared to ramp quickly on Microsoft Fabric Lakehouse/Warehouse, Data Factory, and Power BI as the target platform, while bringing strong transferable production experience from AWS, Oracle/Redshift-style warehousing, Python/SQL, and financial services data.
conclusion: I would welcome the opportunity to help Hilltop Securities build a reliable risk analytics data foundation that improves transparency, reporting speed, and data-driven decision-making.
